title: US Said It 'Decimated' Iran's Nuclear Program. Satellite Images Tell a Different Story.
narrative: The US government says it has decimated Iran's nuclear capabilities through months of strikes on universities, enrichment facilities, and supply chain nodes. Secretary of War Pete Hegseth declared the administration "prepared to deliver whatever the president expects." National Security Strategy briefs state the strikes "significantly degraded" the program. President Trump warned of a "massive Armada" heading toward the Middle East. Iran has not granted IAEA inspectors access since the strikes, leaving satellite imagery as the only independent verification window.
telemetry: SAT-NATANZ: Fresh construction over previously damaged buildings — new roofs erected over enrichment facility structures, imagery captured January 28, 2026 by Planet Labs PBC
SAT-ISFAHAN: Same pattern — new coverings erected over previously damaged uranium gas production facility by late January 2026
SAT-DAMAGE-VERIFICATION: Images from December 3–7, 2025 confirmed rubble and structural damage at both Natanz and Isfahan — construction began after that window
IRAN-NAVAL: BBC Verify documented minimum 11 Iranian naval vessels destroyed or damaged since early March 2026, including IRINS Makran, Iran's largest drone-carrier vessel
IRGC-STRIKE: Satellite imagery confirmed damage to at least 6 structures at IRGC headquarters in Tehran following strikes
IAEA-ACCESS: Iran has denied IAEA inspectors access to affected nuclear sites since the strikes — no on-ground verification possible
analysis:
The gap between official sources and the physical record defines this story. The narrative being promoted — The US government says it has decimated Iran's nuclear capabilities through months of strikes on universities, enrichment facilities, and supply chain nodes. Secretary of War Pete Hegseth declared the... — is not fully supported by the available telemetry.

Key data points from the physical record:
  1. SAT-NATANZ: Fresh construction over previously damaged buildings — new roofs erected over enrichment facility structures, imagery captured January 28, 2026 by Planet Labs PBC
  2. SAT-ISFAHAN: Same pattern — new coverings erected over previously damaged uranium gas production facility by late January 2026
  3. SAT-DAMAGE-VERIFICATION: Images from December 3–7, 2025 confirmed rubble and structural damage at both Natanz and Isfahan — construction began after that window
  4. IRAN-NAVAL: BBC Verify documented minimum 11 Iranian naval vessels destroyed or damaged since early March 2026, including IRINS Makran, Iran's largest drone-carrier vessel
  5. IRGC-STRIKE: Satellite imagery confirmed damage to at least 6 structures at IRGC headquarters in Tehran following strikes
  6. IAEA-ACCESS: Iran has denied IAEA inspectors access to affected nuclear sites since the strikes — no on-ground verification possible

The official narrative presents one version of events. The telemetry — direct data points, independently verifiable records, and observable physical evidence — points in a different direction or raises questions the official account doesn't address.

[SIPHONED VERDICT]: US officials claim Iran's nuclear program is "decimated" while satellite imagery shows active reconstruction at the two facilities Israel and the US struck first — and no IAEA inspector has set foot inside either site to contradict the before-and-after narrative.

The question is not whether the official account is false. The question is whether the evidence supports the account as stated, or whether the gap between narrative and data tells a more complete story.
verdict: [SIPHONED VERDICT]: US officials claim Iran's nuclear program is "decimated" while satellite imagery shows active reconstruction at the two facilities Israel and the US struck first — and no IAEA inspector has set foot inside either site to contradict the before-and-after narrative.
